cross_scene_arcs.title: Control Over Narrative and Performance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Andy consistently asserts control over how his story and performances are presented, from framing his biopic to setting conditions for his sitcom role and revealing orchestrated deceptions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Andy begins narrating his life story on his own terms, rejecting a false version of the film
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Andy confirms his orchestration of the Tony Clifton persona as part of an artistic hoax, asserting creative authority
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the extent to which his manipulations are ethically justified as art remains contested

cross_scene_arcs.title: Struggle for Audience Connection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Andy repeatedly faces challenges in connecting with audiences, from childhood performances to live venues and television, often meeting confusion, indifference, or disengagement.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Andy performs for imaginary audiences until his father insists real people are necessary
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Andy completes a reading to a nearly empty, sleeping audience and exits unnoticed
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether artistic integrity can exist without audience recognition or response

cross_scene_arcs.title: Professional Ascent and Representation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Andy transitions from obscurity to professional recognition, gaining representation and negotiating high-profile roles while maintaining artistic control.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Andy performs publicly in character for the first time, struggling to connect
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Andy agrees to star in a network sitcom under self-defined conditions, including inclusion of Tony Clifton
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about whether the industry will accept his unconventional demands

cross_scene_arcs.title: Personal Relationships and Emotional Isolation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Andy's difficulty with emotional intimacy and authenticity strains personal relationships, culminating in a diagnosis that tests trust and care among friends.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Andy's abrupt marriage proposal unsettles Mimi, leading to emotional withdrawal
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Andy reveals a cancer diagnosis, met with concern from Lynne and skepticism from George
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: George's doubt about the truth of the diagnosis reveals ongoing tension between Andy's performance and personal authenticity

cross_scene_arcs.title: Escalating Psychological Strain
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Andy exhibits signs of psychological distress, from compulsive behavior to the revelation of a serious illness, blurring the line between performance and personal crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Andy engages in manic, repetitive handwashing, indicating anxiety or obsession
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Andy discloses a lung cancer diagnosis, merging private suffering with public persona
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: source of Andy's anxiety and the authenticity of his illness remain partially unverified and emotionally charged

cross_scene_arcs.title: Architect of Deception
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Zmuda evolves from behind-the-scenes advisor to active performer in Andy's pranks, advocating for long-term manipulation of audience trust and embracing the Tony Clifton role as a core part of the hoax.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: strategic advisor pushing Andy to stay on the show to build audience trust
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: confirmed as the performer under the Tony Clifton makeup, enthusiastic about continuing the deception
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: ethical ambiguity of deceiving audiences and friends

cross_scene_arcs.title: Shifting Power in Partnership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The dynamic between Zmuda and Andy shifts from mutual collaboration to increasing tension and imbalance, marked by physical coercion and revelation of hidden truths that erode trust.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: equal creative partners in performance hoaxes
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Zmuda becomes unsettled by Andy's duplicity and growing control, questioning the authenticity of their shared reality
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: reconciling Andy's public vulnerability with private behavior

cross_scene_arcs.title: Protector Amid Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Zmuda transitions from observer to active caretaker during moments of physical and emotional crisis, asserting control over Andy's environment before being excluded from his medical journey.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: assumes responsibility for Andy's safety after the wrestling injury
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: removed from the spa and distanced from Andy's care, despite continued loyalty
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how to support Andy when barred from treatment settings

cross_scene_arcs.title: Performance vs. Reality in Sickness
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Zmuda struggles to reconcile Andy's cancer diagnosis with his own tendency to treat serious events as material for performance, ultimately confronting the limits of artifice in the face of real illness and deception.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: immediately frames Andy's cancer as a potential act
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: emotionally overwhelmed, accepting the futility of treatment and Andy's embrace of absurdity
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether Zmuda believes the diagnosis or suspects it as another hoax

cross_scene_arcs.title: Witness to the Final Act
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Zmuda moves from enabling Andy's experiences to silently bearing witness to his death, participating in the ambiguity of Andy's final performance and sharing grief with others left uncertain.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: guides Andy through morally ambiguous experiences like visiting a brothel
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: stands in quiet mourning at the funeral, embracing ambiguity and shared loss
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about how to process Andy's death and the staged nature of the event

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Skeptic to Agent: George's Commitment to Andy's Talent
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: George evolves from a curious observer to a committed agent after recognizing the intentionality behind Andy's eccentric performance, culminating in his decision to represent him despite initial doubts.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: intrigued by Andy's act and motivated to learn more
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: convinced that Andy's unusual behavior reflects intentional artistry rather than mere oddness
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how to market an act that defies conventional formats

cross_scene_arcs.title: Navigating Deception: George's Relationship with the Tony Clifton Persona
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: George experiences confusion, confrontation, and eventual complicity around the Tony Clifton hoax, shifting from being a target of the prank to protecting its secrecy, only to be publicly challenged by its ongoing presentation as real.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: rattled and disturbed after being singled out by Tony Clifton
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: emotionally overwhelmed upon seeing the Tony Clifton identity publicly treated as separate from Andy
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the performance may further entrench the false separation between Kaufman and Clifton

cross_scene_arcs.title: Guardian of Truth Amid Performance and Illness
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: George repeatedly confronts the boundary between reality and performance, from questioning Andy's cancer diagnosis to grappling with the ambiguity of his death, positioning himself as a skeptic seeking truth in a world of artifice.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: skeptical of Andy's cancer claim and demanding confirmation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: acknowledges Andy's absence while recognizing the lingering uncertainty surrounding his death
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether Andy's death is real or part of an ongoing performance

cross_scene_arcs.title: Managerial Tensions: Artistic Vision vs. Audience Expectations
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: George challenges Andy's artistic choices when they alienate audiences or rely on questionable beliefs, asserting his role as a pragmatic manager who questions whether the act serves the audience or only the performer.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: angry about audience walkouts and regional mismatch
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: moves from silent disapproval to cautious engagement despite doubts about Andy's plans
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether George will continue to challenge Andy's beliefs or go along with his plans despite doubts
